response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine Staff-level pedigree across a long polyglot career, and his current role at re:cinq — working on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks — maps directly to the AI-native engineering environment this role demands. His Dunnhumby tenure demonstrates credible high-throughput distributed systems experience at real scale (millions of HTTP requests per second). However, Python is listed as a must-have and is entirely absent from his resume and skill list, which is a material gap that cannot be inferred away. His distributed systems depth is also six years stale, and the AI-agent work at re:cinq is only weeks old, making it impossible to assess impact or depth.
strengths: Current hands-on work building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq aligns precisely with the AI-native engineering environment the role requires
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and high-performance data pipelines
Consistent Staff-level titling across multiple companies with evidence of technical leadership, craftsmanship advocacy (TDD, pairing, trunk-based development), and community knowledge-sharing
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume and skills list despite being explicitly listed as a must-have technical requirement
Distributed systems depth is concentrated in a role from 2018–2020 using DLang; more recent work is TypeScript product and tooling without clear high-throughput systems scope
AI-agent infrastructure experience at re:cinq began April 2025 — too recent to assess depth, ownership, or measurable impact
